instructions.how_to_review: 将你想要发布的活动的 'selected' 字段改为 true
instructions.next_step: 保存文件后运行: npm run generate-post [此文件路径]

fields_explanation.title: 活动标题
fields_explanation.priority: 优先级分数 (越高越重要)

events.title: San Valentine's Cruise Party • Singles & Couples @ City Cruises SF
events.location: Pier 3 Hornblower Landing, San Francisco, CA 94111
events.description_preview: San Valentines Cruise Party is the perfect ship to start Valentine's celebration Switch up your Vale...
events.description_detail:
San Valentines Cruise Party  is the perfect ship to start Valentine's celebration  
Switch up your Valentines' routine and join us
San Valentines Cruise Party is the perfect ship to celebrate love, friendship and future relationships. For your Valentine's festivities in San Francisco.
Switch up your Valentines' routine and join us on one of the most fun love cruises of the year. Dance with your love, friends to top hits mixed by our live band and DJ'S on this cruise around the Bay.
Enjoy refreshments and cocktails at our 7 bars, and sightsee our amazing cities on this exciting and magical night!
• 3 hours cruise w/ breathtaking views of San Francisco Bay by night
• 4 Floors of Music/ 4 Live bands ( Live music from Colombia with the spectacular Sonora Dinamita -  live Salsa with Orquesta Rafa y los ejecutivos - La Nueva Herrencia and Live Mariachi with Mariachi Juarez, one more room with the best Top 40/ 70'/ 80's/ 90's plus 8 of the best Bay Area djs )
* First Floor with La Sonora Dinamita
* Second floor with Banda La Nueva Herencia
* Third Floor with Orquesta Rafa y los ejecutivos
* Fourth Floor Top 40/ 70'/ 80's/ 90's
* Live Mariachi with Mariachi San Francisco
* Cruise San Francisco Bell
• Guests enjoy spectacular views of Golden Gate, Bay Bridge,Treasure Island, Alcatraz & more!
• Classic river boat built in 1994 in Louisiana with the inspiration from the elegant riverboats of the 1800’s
• The biggest boat in San Francisco - 1,500 guest capacity (Cruise San Francisco Bell)
• 7 fully stocked Bars and food stations
• Multiple enclosed outdoor decks
• Bottle service available
• The ticket give you access to all the rooms (4 rooms with live music)
City Cruises & Events - Pier 3 Hornblower Landing / San Francisco ca 94111
Info: (650) 281-5391 / (408) 250-3694
Check in time begins at 7:30pm - Boarding begin 8.00pm and boat depart at 9.15pm sharp.

This Valentine’s season, slow down and savor the pleasures worth loving, exceptional wine, thoughtful cuisine, and the art of pairing them beautifully.
Join us for an intimate, sommelier-led, micro Aphrodisiac Wine Pairing designed to awaken the senses and celebrate love in its most refined forms, taste, craftsmanship, and shared experience.

events.title: Valentine’s Day Celebration Dinner & Dance at The Cats
events.location: 17533 Santa Cruz Highway, Los Gatos, CA 95033
events.description_preview: A Valentine’s Celebration Dinner & Dance with a 4-course menu, individual entrées, music, dancing, a...
events.description_detail:
A Valentine’s Celebration Dinner & Dance with a 4-course menu, individual entrées, music, dancing, and reserved seating. Limited seating.
4-Course Dinner • Dancing • Music📍 The Cats — Celebration HQ🗓️ Saturday Night Only
Valentine’s Day is a celebration — not just a dinner.
Join us for a ticketed Valentine’s Celebration Dinner & Dance featuring a 4-course plated dinner, individual entrées, and a high-energy dance floor powered by guest DJ Joe Carnnizzaro, one of the area’s favorite local DJs.
Perfect for couples and double dates who want great food, great vibes, and time to celebrate together.
🎟️ Advance tickets required | Limited seating🎉 What’s Included
✔️ 4-course Valentine’s Celebration Dinner✔️ Individual entrée selection (per guest)✔️ Dessert included✔️ Dance floor with live DJ Joe Carnnizzaro✔️ Curated Valentine’s playlist + couple song requests✔️ Full bar available
First CourseArugula, Avocado Salad with Champagne Vinaigrette
Second CourseMiso-Glazed Salmon with Jasmine Rice
Main Course – Choose One
DessertUbe Cake with Chocolate Sauce & Strawberry Ice Cream
Celebrate all night with ongoing champagne pours served tableside during your dinner seating.Limited availability.
Dance before dinner, after dinner, or whenever the vibe hits.
⏰ Early Celebration Dinner: 5:00 – 7:00 PM⏰ Late Celebration Dinner: 7:30 – 9:30 PM
Please arrive within 15 minutes of your selected time.
Order by Jan. 31st and get $10 off. (Promo Code "EarlyValentine")
The Cats Restaurant & BarLos Gatos | Historic venue | Full bar | Music

events.title: Valentines Day 7:00 PM | Grapes & Giggles
events.location: 1697 Industrial Rd., San Carlos, CA 94070
events.description_preview: Stand-up comedy at the iconic Domenico Winery! You no longer have to travel to San Jose or San Franc...
events.description_detail:
Stand-up comedy at the iconic Domenico Winery! You no longer have to travel to San Jose or San Francisco for comedy! Stay in the Peninsula.
Stand-up comedy at the iconic Domenico Winery! You no longer have to travel to San Jose or San Francisco for comedy! Stay in the Peninsula.
Comedy and wine in a beautiful venue on the Peninsula every month!
See comedians featured on late-night TV, Comedy Central, SF Sketchfest, Bay Area Festivals, and more!
Before the show, come early and dine in the Osteria. Give yourself an hour to enjoy dinner at this playful eatery and wine bar, drawing inspiration from an aspiring post-war Italy. Dinner reservations before the show are strongly encouraged! Click here to book a table. Food and beverages from the Osteria may not be brought into the event.
During the show, you can order wine, beer, cocktails, bar bites, and dessert from your server or a QR code, and we will deliver it to your table! This helps reduce talking during the show so you don't miss a joke.
Show Starts at 7:00 pm (ends around 8:15 pm) | Doors Open at 6:30 pm
Coming with a group? Plan ahead and purchase your tickets in one order, or we cannot guarantee your party will be seated at the same table. For large groups, we have round tables of 6 - 10 guests. You can purchase a table with at least 6 tickets, then add single tickets a la carte, and your friends can sit at your table.
Check out the event at 9:00 PM if that fits your plans better!
21+ Event. 24-hour notice is required for refunds (less Eventbrite fees), or credits for a future show.
Check out Grapes & Giggles for more shows. If you live in the Peninsula, this is the show for you. Are you looking for things to do? This is the #1 fun night out. We've had attendees from Redwood City, San Mateo, Belmont, Pacifica, Foster City, Palo Alto, Burlingame, South San Francisco, Menlo Park, Daly City, Mountain View, Los Altos, Sunnyvale, Millbrae, and, of course, San Carlos!
Refunds up to 7 days before event

events.title: LOVE BITES!
events.location: 1 Ferry Building, San Francisco, CA 94111
events.description_preview: Food and wine tasting at the San Francisco Ferry Building. Food and wine tasting at the San Francisc...
events.description_detail:
Food and wine tasting at the San Francisco Ferry Building.
Food and wine tasting at the San Francisco Ferry Building.
Eat and drink your way around the marketplace with small bites and wine tastings.
This event proceeds benefit Foodwise, the nonprofit operator of the Ferry Plaza Farmers Market and education programs for eaters of all ages.
Anthem Winery And Vineyards, Bjorn Vineyards, Brandlin Estate, Cuvaison, Gen 7 Wines, Gibbs Vineyards, Goosecross Cellars, Grgich Hills Estate, Hossfeld Vineyards, JAX Vineyards, Markham Vineyards, Oakville Ranch Vineyards, Parallel Napa Valley, Priest Ranch, Prime Solum, Round Pond Estate, Rudd Estate, STEREOGRAPH, Trinitas Cellars, Merryvale Vineyards
A16 La Pala, Acme Bread Company, EPIC Steak, Epicurean Trader, Far West Fungi, Fatted Calf, Ferry Plaza Wine Merchant, Fort Point Beer Company, Gott's Roadside, Heath Ceramics, Hog Island Oyster Co., Juice House Co., Lunette Cambodia, Mariposa Baking Co., Miette Patisserie, Nash Bakes, NOPA Fish, Obour Foods, One Hotel Terrene, Ozumo, Recchiuti Confections, Tsar Nicoulai Caviar, Village Market.
While there is no entrance fee, attendees must purchase 'tasting tickets' to exchange for bites and sips at the event. Eventbrite registrations include 10 Tasting Tickets and a signature wine glass. Additional Tasting Tickets may be purchased at the event. Walkups welcome while supplies last! Wineries and merchant bite stations will be located throughout the marketplace on the first floor of the Ferry Building.
Support local San Francisco businesses and join the fun IRL at the Ferry Building!
The Ferry Building Marketplace is located along the Embarcadero at the foot of Market Street. It is the center of a transit hub that connects all of San Francisco’s neighborhoods and the surrounding bay area communities. The Ferry Building Marketplace is accessible by MUNI, BART, and ferry. The historic trolley cars (F Line Market) stop directly in front of the Ferry Building.

events.description_detail:
Y’s Choice Presents…Bites, Brushes & Bottles
💖 Bites, Brushes & Bottles: A Galentine’s Day Celebration 🎨🥂
Grab your girls and join us for a night of creativity, culture, and connection at Bites, Brushes & Bottles, a Galentine’s Day experience you won’t want to miss!
Hosted by Y’s Choice in collaboration with Pursuit of Peace Painting, this vibrant Paint & Sip-style event blends art, soulful bites, bubbly bottles, and good vibes, all in one unforgettable evening.
Whether you’re celebrating friendship, self-love, or just need a fun night out, this Galentine’s gathering is all about joy, laughter, and creating something beautiful together.
🎟️ Tickets: $75Includes food, drinks, paint & supplies, music, and gift

📅 Date & Time:Thursday, February 126:00 PM – 9:00 PM
💡 Come ready to sip, paint, eat, and vibe. Seats are limited, so secure your spot early!
No refunds. All sales are final.

events.title: Noite de Carnaval: A Celebration of Joy
events.location: 1100 California Street, San Francisco, CA 94108
events.description_preview: You're invited to Grace Cathedral’s annual fundraising gala, Noite de Carnaval: A Celebration of Joy...
events.description_detail:
You're invited to Grace Cathedral’s annual fundraising gala, Noite de Carnaval: A Celebration of Joy, on February 13, 2026!
Brazilian Carnaval is a dazzling expression of connection, community, and creativity-a collective celebration that transcends individuality and honors our shared humanity. Through music, dance, and spectacle, it reflects beauty and resilience, hope and possibility, reminding us that joy endures and anything is possible.
At Grace Cathedral, our celebration is fueled by the same aspirations. Like Carnaval in Brazil, this evening encourages us to step beyond ourselves, to celebrate together, and to imagine a world shaped by compassion, courage, and shared delight.
Join us for an extraordinary night where joy becomes a collective act, creativity shines, and our community gathers in jubilation.
The night doesn’t end with dinner! After the gala, the celebration turns up at The Late Night Revelry—a high-energy dance party inside our festively transformed chapel. Bring your friends and experience this party in the chapel!
Want to skip dinner and get straight to the after-party? Separate tickets are available for attendance at The Late Night Revelry, which unlocks access to the DJ, bites, and open bar!
Learn more about our mission, the event, or get tickets directly through our site at gracecathedral.org/carnivale.
You're invited to Grace Cathedral’s annual fundraising gala, Noite de Carnaval: A Celebration of Joy, on February 13, 2026!
Brazilian Carnaval is a dazzling expression of connection, community, and creativity-a collective celebration that transcends individuality and honors our shared humanity. Through music, dance, and spectacle, it reflects beauty and resilience, hope and possibility, reminding us that joy endures and anything is possible.
At Grace Cathedral, our celebration is fueled by the same aspirations.

events.title: Valentine's Day Swing Dance on the historic USS Hornet
events.location: 707 West Hornet Avenue, Alameda, CA 94501
events.description_preview: The USS Hornet is opening up its bulkheads and hatches for the second annual 1940’s Valentine's swin...
events.description_detail:
The USS Hornet is opening up its bulkheads and hatches for the second annual 1940’s Valentine's swing dance on February 14, 2026.
Picture the romance of a WWII sailor returning from war to his sweetheart…and now come recreate this feeling aboard the USS Hornet aircraft carrier on Valentines Day.
The USS Hornet is opening up its bulkheads and hatches for a 1940’s swing dance on February 14. This evening will feature drinks, dancing and a whole lot of nostalgia and romance. The Bay Area’s unavoidably swingin’ big band, the Death and Taxes Swing Band will provide an evening of great music for dancing or listening. Come as a couple or come to meet someone!
At 6:30 doors will open for drinks and appetizers, and then at 6:45 there will be a short introduction about the history of the ship. At 7:00 there will be a short dance lesson and then the Death and Taxes Swing Band will take you back to the 1940’s until around 9:00.
Tickets are $30 in advance, $35 at the door.
This is an 18+ age event. No children allowed.

events.title: 4th Annual Shrimp and Grits Taste Off
events.location: 1000 Oak Street, Oakland, CA 94607
events.description_preview: The 4th Annual Shrimp and Grits Taste Off is back and bigger than ever, bringing together bold flavo...
events.description_detail:
The 4th Annual Shrimp and Grits Taste Off is back and bigger than ever, bringing together bold flavors, local talent, and great vibes.
Get ready for a really good time. The Shrimp and Grits Taste Off at Town Fare is a high-energy, flavor-packed celebration of one iconic dish—Bay Area style. This beloved culinary showdown invites some of the region’s most creative chefs to put their own spin on the classic Southern dish—think rich, buttery grits, perfectly seasoned shrimp, and unexpected twists that keep every bite exciting. Guests will sample a wide range of interpretations, vote for their favorites, and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere that makes Town Fare a true community gathering space.
Local chefs bring their A-game, serving up soulful, inventive, and downright crave-worthy shrimp and grits that showcase their unique culinary voices. Add great drinks, a buzzing crowd, and the unbeatable setting of Town Fare, and you’ve got an event that feels equal parts food festival and party. Whether you’re a shrimp and grits purist or love a creative twist, this is the place to eat, sip, vibe, and cheer on your favorite chef. Come for the food, stay for the fun, and leave full and happy.
The 4th Annual Shrimp and Grits Taste Off is back and bigger than ever, bringing together bold flavors, local talent, and great vibes.

events.description_detail:
You and yours will each make FOUR festive cocktails using real bar tools.  Per person price includes gratuity and sales tax.  Unique, fun!
Real glassware provides a level of elegance for your Valentines selfies. You'll learn 4 special cocktails, along with garnishes. Lots of entertaining bartending tips and stories, too.
Real cocktail shakers, strainers, muddlers, and spirits bottles. You will not find this much fun by going to a restaurant... nor will you find 2.5 hours of fun at this price with tax and gratuity included!
On this one night only per year, our renown make-your-own pizza experience will be included in the price of your Valentines Mixology admission. Prices are per person. NO REFUNDS, No Drop-ins. Space is limited, so please register early. Valentines is on a Saturday night in 2026.
City of Cocktail is run by a former schoolteacher and a professional bartender. Immense materials preparation and the Bay Area's most unique team building event site: a hand-built concrete countertop event space and wood-fired pizza oven.

events.title: Gelato Class | Gelato Tiramisu
events.location: 2855 Stevens Creek Boulevard, San Jose, CA 95128
events.description_preview: Celebrate the season of love (and dessert) with your partner for Valentine’s Day with a hand-on gela...
events.description_detail:
Celebrate the season of love (and dessert) with your partner for Valentine’s Day with a hand-on gelato tiramisu experience.
Celebrate the season of love (and dessert) with your partner for Valentine’s Day with a hand-on gelato tiramisu experience. In this class you will create a gelato tiramisu with classic Valentine’s Day flavors and colors. Perfect for enjoying with your sweetheart. Learn all about the traditions of gelato making and work to craft a unique holiday treat to enjoy!
About Patrizia Pasqualetti:
Gelato Chef Patrizia Pasqualetti shares the art of authentic Italian gelato made with natural and local ingredients. Originally from the Umbria region of Italy, Patrizia's artisanal creations are some of our favorites at Eataly. Patrizia showcases her passion, love, and generational gelato expertise with options for every lifestyle: gluten-free, dairy-free, and vegan.
In this Class You Will:
This class will take place on the 2nd floor in the la Scuola classroom
Payment in full is required at the time of registration and all sales are final. No refunds are offered on classes and events. For groups of 5-10 guests, a minimum of 7 days' notice (class day not included) is required to receive a credit in the form of a coupon for a future class or event. For groups of up to 4 guests, a minimum of 4 days' notice (class day not included) is required to receive a credit in the form of a coupon code for a future class or event at La Scuola. We reserve the right to reschedule or cancel a class or event due to unforeseen circumstances, inclement weather or otherwise. In the event of a class or event cancellation, we will contact you directly at the phone number or email address you have provided to offer a full refund or credit to transfer to a future class or event.

Villa Succulents is a local, women-owned business led by Ana Villa. During this class we will be designing your own orchid & succulent planter. events.title: Innovating for Massively Better Healthcare!
events.location: Byers Auditorium in UCSF's Genentech Hall 600 16th StreetSan Francisco, California 94158
events.description_preview: UCSF Innovation Ventures hosts visionary health transformers Halle Tecco, Sean Duffy, Jeff Tangney, ...
events.description_detail: UCSF Innovation Ventures hosts visionary health transformers Halle Tecco, Sean Duffy, Jeff Tangney, and Adam Gazzaley!Meet legendary 'Digital Health trailblazers' - Halle Tecco, Author of Massively Better Healthcare, Sean Duffy, CEO of Omada Health, Jeff Tangney, CEO of Doximity, and Adam Gazzaley, Founder of Neuroscape Lab and Co-Founder/CSA of Akili Interactive.Learn about the evolution of the Digital Health field from the co-Founder of Rock Health, Halle Tecco. Hear first-hand accounts from Sean and Jeff on how local digital health innovations became industry leaders and publicly traded companies. Understand the essential role of UCSF frontline Innovators as Adam Gazzaley recounts the journey of Akili to become the first FDA approved Digital Therapeutic.UCSF Innovation Ventures is honored to host these distinguished pioneers in healthcare transformation for an in-person exploration of how to strategically innovate, intentionally navigate healthcare's complexity, build sustainable solutions, and create lasting change in one of society's most vital systems. Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur, healthcare professional, or changemaker, this conversation offers actionable insights for anyone passionate about improving healthcare outcomes.Also gain a sneak preview of Halle's new book, Massively Better Healthcare. Drawing from her 15+ years as a healthcare entrepreneur, investor, and educator, Tecco will share her insider's guide to transforming our broken healthcare system through strategic innovation.Join us in envisioning a new landscape of "Healthcare for Each and for All!"Special Thanks to Rock Health, JHU Bloomberg School of Public Health, and Women in Bio for collaborating on this exciting event!Read moreCategory: Science & Tech, Medicine

events.title: The Alabama Solution: A Special Film Screening & Conversation
events.location: 3200 Grand Avenue, Oakland, CA 94610
events.description_preview: Join us for a thought-provoking screening and dynamic discussion on The Alabama Solution, featuring ...
events.description_detail:
Join us for a thought-provoking screening and dynamic discussion on The Alabama Solution, featuring leaders across criminal justice.
The program opens with a special screening of HBO’s acclaimed documentary The Alabama Solution, a gripping inside look at how incarcerated men risk everything to expose injustice—using footage filmed behind prison walls.
In the spirit of the film’s title, the evening continues with a post-screening discussion focused on solutions. Leaders from prosecution, corrections, reentry, and reform will explore what real, tangible change can look like—and how it can be achieved.
Doors open at 5pm. Screening begins at 5:30pm. Panel ends at 8pm.
Content Warning: The film contains graphic depictions of violence and death and is not appropriate for children.
Safety Reminder: Please do not attend if you are experiencing cold or flu symptoms. Masks will be available at the registration desk.

events.title: 2026 Chinese Spring Festival Culture Fair - Free Event
events.location: 255 South Almaden Boulevard, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description_preview: 2026年春节文化集市，免费嗨翻天，带你感受最地道的年味儿！ Free entry for all! 2026 Chinese Spring Festival Culture Fair is your...
events.description_detail:
2026年春节文化集市，免费嗨翻天，带你感受最地道的年味儿！
Free entry for all! 2026 Chinese Spring Festival Culture Fair is your Year of the Horse celebration—fast, fearless, and full of festive energy. Join us in person to experience the Spring Festival up close, where tradition meets hands-on fun: create a Year of the Horse lantern, sculpt a colorful clay pony, and bring horse-themed blessings to life through crafts you can take home.
Dive into the maker zone with family-friendly favorites: build a spinning Zoetrope animation tube, craft a dazzling kaleidoscope, fold a bright pinwheel, and try classic New Year print / tile rubbing with ink on red paper—instant “new year vibes,” guaranteed.
For culture lovers, we’ve got a full tradition lineup: dress up for Hanfu photo moments, write your own Fu (福) with calligraphy, solve lantern riddles, and watch live sugar painting turn syrup into art in seconds.
And don’t miss the Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) highlights—made for the Year of the Horse spotlight: create a one-of-a-kind lacquer fan (漆扇) with flowing marbled textures, and craft an elegant Song Brocade wind chime (宋锦风铃) where heritage fabric meets the sound of spring.
Want action? Jump into classic games like diabolo, pitch-pot, kick shuttlecock, and ring toss, plus a high-energy kids’ whack-a-mole challenge—along with face painting, balloon art, and close-up magic surprises. Bring your friends and family, celebrate the Year of the Horse together, and leave with handmade treasures, new skills, and unforgettable Spring Festival joy.
白天来逛免费集市，和家人朋友一起把年味儿“亲手做出来”：做马年灯笼、捏彩色小马粘土，再到手作区体验 Zoetrope 动画纸筒、万花筒、折纸风车、年画/瓦当拓印；喜欢非遗的一定别错过 漆扇与宋锦风铃——把传统工艺的质感和新年的灵气带回家。现场还有汉服拍照、书法写福、灯谜互动、糖画、以及抖空竹、投壶、踢毽子、套圈等传统游戏，热闹到停不下来。
更重要的是：晚上同一地点的剧场，还有一场真正的“过年大团圆”——传统春节联欢晚会。这不仅是一场演出，更是一场万人同频的线下大团圆：熟悉的乡音、惊艳的才艺、不绝的掌声与笑声，都将在这里真实发生。节目形式丰富：传统舞蹈、现代舞蹈、歌曲、脱口秀、小品、舞台剧、大提琴、民乐等多种表演一晚看过瘾。晚会时间：7:00–9:30 PM（6:00 PM 开始入场）
zgzg.li/2026-ticket-eb
2026年春节文化集市，免费嗨翻天，带你感受最地道的年味儿！
Free entry for all!

events.title: Volunteer Farm Chores
events.location: 14320 Arnerich Road, Los Gatos, CA 95020
events.description_preview: Come join us at Ma'alot Farms in Los Gatos! We will get our hands dirty, have fun, enjoy farm snacks...
events.description_detail:
Come join us at Ma'alot Farms in Los Gatos! We will get our hands dirty, have fun, enjoy farm snacks and help our community
Come join us at Ma'alot Farms in Los Gatos! We will get our hands dirty, have fun, enjoy farm snacks and help our community!
Volunteer Farm Chores
Life on a farm is constantly changing with the seasons, and our volunteer days reflect that. Come join us as we work the land in order to produce bountiful harvests that we can share with the larger community.
Each volunteer session will consist of at least two different tasks, based on the needs of the farm that day. This might include planting seeds in the greenhouse, transplanting seedlings into the garden, thinning the plant starts in the garden, weeding, mulching, and harvesting.
Please come ready to get dirty. We recommend clothes that you're okay with getting dirty/muddy, sturdy closed-toe shoes or boots, a sun hat, and a water bottle. Snacks will be provided.
We are happy to validate volunteer hours for schools and community organzations.
We look forward to seeing you on the farm!
Nurturing the Earth, Nourishing Communities, Growing a Sustainable Future
Ma’alot Farms is cultivating a healthier world through sustainable farming, regenerative practices and community-centered food systems.
One of the ways we foster community here at Ma’alot Farms is through our Volunteer Farm Activities. Working at the farm provides volunteers an opportunity to experience life at the farm, get to know one another in smaller groups, gain skills to grow their own food, and assist in the desperately needed hunger relief work in Silicon Valley.
At Ma’alot Farms, we donate the vast majority of the produce we grow. The work of our volunteers contributes mightily in our ability to help feed a growing food-insecure community in Silicon Valley with high-quality, nutritious food produced in sustainable and regenerative ways.

events.title: Love’s Greeting: An Intimate Valentine’s Evening with Harp & Violin
events.location: 230 San Antonio Circle, Mountain View, CA 94040
events.description_preview: Celebrate Valentine’s Day with Love’s Greeting, an intimate concert designed for shared listening an...
events.description_detail:
Celebrate Valentine’s Day with Love’s Greeting, an intimate concert designed for shared listening and quiet connection.
Featuring live harp and violin, the evening pairs warm, romantic music with subtle multimedia visuals to create a gentle, atmospheric experience. Rather than a traditional recital, the concert invites you to slow down and enjoy music together—in a space shaped by sound, light, and presence.Without revealing too much, the concert incorporates new approaches to the interplay of sound and visuals, offering a listening experience that unfolds differently each time.
Perfect for couples seeking a meaningful Valentine’s outing, while also welcoming anyone who wishes to spend the evening immersed in beautiful live music.
Seating is general admission. Attendees are encouraged to RSVP to reserve the space.
Performances and screenings in Tateuchi Hall are suitable for ages 7 and up. To ensure the best experience for all guests, children should be able to sit quietly for the duration of the performance (approximately one hour).

events.title: Friday the 13th Whimsy Market at Devil's Canyon
events.location: 935 Washington Street, San Carlos, CA 94070
events.description_preview: Join us for an evening of witchy artisan shopping, tarot readings, henna, crystal hunting, and On Fr...
events.description_detail:
Join us for an evening of witchy artisan shopping, tarot readings, henna, crystal hunting, and
On Friday, February 13th, come prepared to indulge in local, handmade artisan goods of a metaphysical and witchy nature.
Plan to spend a couple of hours:
This will be the perfect evening to get together with your friends. There will be food trucks, music, wine, beer, and 40 vendors to shop from. Devil's Canyon Brewing will have their heating lamps on in the beer garden, and there will be indoor seating. Vendors will be both inside and outside, so make sure to check them all out.
Shopping will be from 4-9 p.m. at Devil's Canyon Brewing. Plan to park a couple of blocks away and walk.
Please reserve your ticket(s) so we can get an idea of how many people are coming. Invite your friends and family to join in on the fun! Hope to see you there.
Follow us on Instagram or Facebook @thewhimsymarket for updates and product posts. We will have an event discussion dedicated to vendor product posts on Facebook.

events.title: Script Off Live: Star Power Stars vs AI
events.location: 850 Woodside Way, San Mateo, CA 94401
events.description_preview: SAMCAR Professional Development live Script Off challenge with Alexis Bolin, Gregg Neuman, moderated...
events.description_detail:
SAMCAR Professional Development live Script Off challenge with Alexis Bolin, Gregg Neuman, moderated by Samia Morgan with AI.
The SAMCAR Professional Development Committee invites you to a dynamic "Script Off" event where real estate legends Alexis Bolin, "The Queen of Scripts," and Gregg Neuman, "The King of Closings," will go head-to-head against AI in a live, in-person challenge. Moderated by Samia Morgan, this unique session pits veteran expertise against artificial intelligence to tackle real-world scripts, complex scenarios, and industry questions in real-time. While attendance is free, this special presentation is held in proud support of the Kilimanjaro Kidz fundraising effort through Star Power, featuring these "Star Power Stars" whose time was generously secured through a charity auction.
Please submit your questions for the script off before February 2 to samcar@samcar.org
[Disclaimer: By attending, you acknowledge that the presentation is being recorded. Camera angles are intended to capture the presenter only; however, your voice or likeness may be captured if you participate in Q&A or are in view of the camera.]

events.title: Inside the Mind of an Angel Investor
events.location: 2000 Alameda de las Pulgas, San Mateo, CA 94403
events.description_preview: Cracking the Funding Code Hybrid in-person and video presentation by István Szanda What really happe...
events.description_detail:
Cracking the Funding Code
Hybrid in-person and video presentation by István Szanda
What really happens behind closed doors when investors evaluate a pitch? Why do some promising startups get funded while others—equally brilliant—walk away empty-handed?
The Hungarian Innovators Club cordially invites you to a high-impact evening designed to demystify the world of Angel Investing. This is not just a networking event; it is a rare opportunity to gain direct access to the decision-making criteria of Silicon Valley investors.
For Founders and Startups:For entrepreneurs planning to raise capital, understanding the investor’s perspective is not optional—it is a survival skill. This event will equip you with the "insider" knowledge needed to navigate the fundraising landscape with confidence and position your team to survive the scrutiny of professional investors.
For Aspiring Angel Investors:This event is equally indispensable for those considering the transition from operator to investor. If you are thinking of sitting on the other side of the table, you need to know how to look past the hype and assess the true potential of a team. This is your chance to learn the rigorous framework used by seasoned Angels to conduct due diligence, assess risk versus reward, and identify the subtle signals that distinguish a high-growth opportunity from a risky bet.
Cracking the Funding Code
Hybrid in-person and video presentation by István Szanda
What really happens behind closed doors when investors evaluate a pitch? Why do some promising startups get funded while others—equally brilliant—walk away empty-handed?
The Hungarian Innovators Club cordially invites you to a high-impact evening designed to demystify the world of Angel Investing. This is not just a networking event; it is a rare opportunity to gain direct access to the decision-making criteria of Silicon Valley investors.

events.title: Water Tasting, Education, and Appreciation!
events.location: Alcademics, 57 Post Street, Suite 608 San Francisco, CA 94104
events.description_preview: Learn to taste and rate tap and bottled still and sparkling waters like a pro and understand the dif...
events.description_detail: Learn to taste and rate tap and bottled still and sparkling waters like a pro and understand the difference between brands!We’ll taste test 12-16 different waters and students will leave the class with an appreciation for the variety of water to drink, a way to describe how they taste, and a new thirst for this category.Does water have flavor? Is there a difference between tap and bottled water, or from one bottled water to another? Can water express terroir?The answer to all these questions is YES!Come learn about- and taste- lots of water in this class by cocktail writer and educator Camper English. We'll keep your whistle wet this Dry January in this nonalcoholic tasting class.Tasting TapFirst we’ll learn about tap water, including the local stuff in San Francisco and bottled tap waters like Dasani and Aquafina. We’ll briefly discuss home water filters and measure the dissolved solids (TDS) in water with a TDS meter.Is Bottled Water Better?For the majority of the class we'll talk about and taste regionally-sourced bottled waters, learning how to categorize them, how to read their labels, and where to look for highly-rated brands. We’ll learn how waters differ according to type of source, mineral makeup, TDS, pH and more.We’ll taste both bottled still waters and sparkling waters to see the great variation in brands. We’ll look at award-winning waters, waters designed for whiskey pairings, and other novelty water options. We'll taste some brands you've heard of, and several you probably haven't.Location and InstructorClasses are limited to 8 people. Class takes place in an office on the 6th floor of the Mechanics Institute Library building at Post and Market Street in the Financial District, just steps away from the Montgomery BART/MUNI station.Your instructor is Camper English of Alcademics.com. Camper has been writing and teaching about cocktails and spirits in San Francisco for nearly 20 years, and has taught classes on water to more than 200 bartenders and consumers.He is the author of Doctors and Distillers: The Remarkable Medicinal History of Beer, Wine, Spirits, and Cocktails, and The Ice Book: Cool Cubes, Clear Spheres, and Other Chill Cocktail Crafts. Camper has given dozens of talks and classes about cocktails and spirits science and history to audiences of bartenders and consumers. To stay informed about all of Camper's classes, please join the Alcademics Newsletter, which goes out monthly.Want to book a private class? Reach out any time!Read moreCategory: Food & Drink, Other

events.title: IN HER SEASON: WINE CRAWL
events.location: Jackson Street, Oakland, CA 94607
events.description_preview: An elevated wine crawl for women choosing alignment over urgency. Wine, chocolate, brunch & intentio...
events.description_detail:
An elevated wine crawl for women choosing alignment over urgency. Wine, chocolate, brunch & intentional conversation.
Join Girlfriends Tribe for In Her Season: valentine's Day Wine Crawl
In Her Season is an elevated walking wine crawl created for women who are choosing alignment over urgency, presence over pressure, and becoming over comparison.
This experience is for the woman who is learning to slow down, enjoy her life, and focus on herself before rushing into the next chapter.
We will be walking together to two nearby wineries, creating space to connect, laugh, and enjoy the journey , not just the destination.
Our first stop will be light, fun, and social. Our final stop will be where we land ,enjoying brunch, wine, and more intentional conversation around identity, alignment, and honoring the season we are in.
This is a chic, paced, intentional experience for women who are learning to enjoy their becoming.
Because some seasons aren’t about being chosen.
They’re about becoming.
And right now… you’re in your season.
An elevated wine crawl for women choosing alignment over urgency. Wine, chocolate, brunch & intentional conversation.
Join Girlfriends Tribe for In Her Season: valentine's Day Wine Crawl
